findings.annotation_source: Supplementary Table 3 provides DEGs used to annotate Arterioles in prenatal skin. Listed as fine-grained annotation under broad 'Vascular endothelium' category.
findings.trajectory: Arterioles represent the terminal differentiation state along the arteriolar pathway: early endothelial cells -> capillaries -> capillary arterioles -> arterioles (Fig. 4d, Extended Data Fig. 10a-c).
findings.characteristic_genes: GJA5 is identified as the characteristic arteriolar marker gene (Fig. 4d). Sprouting angiogenesis potential ('tip' cell state) is increased in arteriole cells (Extended Data Fig. 10g,h).
findings.macrophage_interactions: Macrophage subsets interact with different subtypes of endothelial cells including arterioles. Aggregated macrophage-endothelial ligand-receptor interactions were assessed (Extended Data Fig. 9a, Supplementary Table 28).
findings.organoid_comparison: Unlike prenatal skin capillary arteriole cells, SkO capillary arteriole cells could not further differentiate into arterioles, indicating immune cells are required for full arteriolar differentiation (Fig. 4d, Extended Data Fig. 10a).
findings.temporal_presence: Endothelial cells including arterioles are present throughout gestation (Fig. 1c, Extended Data Fig. 1d, Supplementary Table 4).
